title: phyllocladus trichomanoides var. alpinus
intro: Phyllocladus trichomanoides var. alpinus, also known as Alpine celery top pine, is a variety of the Phyllocladus trichomanoides tree native to New Zealand. It is a type of coniferous shrub that has been used in traditional Māori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Phyllocladus trichomanoides var. alpinus are said to include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, as well as providing a natural remedy for colds and fever. Herbal preparations of Phyllocladus trichomanoides var. alpinus typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves and stems, which can be consumed as tea or used in topical applications.
